Enhancing radiosensitivity of boron neutron capture therapy for liver cancer with homologous recombination repair inhibitor

evidence_entities.text: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC), particularly in recurrent or treatment-refractory cases, often exhibits poor responsiveness to radiation therapy, increasing the risk of radiation-induced liver disease
Boric acid-mediated boron neutron capture therapy (BA-BNCT) has emerged as a promising approach for liver cancer
We targeted the DNA homologous recombination repair (HRR) protein RAD51. Before neutron irradiation, a RAD51 inhibitor, B02, was administered
inhibition of RAD51 led to increased γH2AX, the DNA double-strand break marker
combining the RAD51 inhibitor B02 with BA-BNCT resulted in tumor cell arrest in the G0/G1 phase
The combination of B02 and BA-BNCT significantly disrupted autophagic flux and promoted apoptosis in the tumor cells
